subject: Public Health
title: Tuberculosis: The Persistent Killer
description: A 60-second lesson on Tuberculosis: The Persistent Killer.

hook.text: TB kills more people than any other single infectious agent—1.3 million per year—yet it's curable.

buildup.text: Mycobacterium tuberculosis spreads through air droplets. One-quarter of humanity carries latent TB infection.

discovery.text: Treatment requires 6 months of multiple antibiotics—patients who stop early drive drug resistance.

twist.text: Multidrug-resistant TB now accounts for over 400,000 cases per year—requiring 2 years of toxic treatment.

climax.text: New shorter regimens and the first new TB drugs in 50 years offer hope—but funding remains inadequate.

punchline.text: TB is curable, preventable, and still the world's deadliest infection.

quiz.question: How many people does tuberculosis kill annually?
